Football.title: Piers Morgan tells Arsenal fans they can still achieve dream quadruple… with Spurs getting relegated
Football.content:
Piers Morgan tells Arsenal fans they can still achieve dream quadruple… with Spurs getting relegated

Arsenal's hopes for a quadruple ain't over until it's over.

Granted, the Gunners may no longer be eligible to win what many would perceive to be the more conventional quadruple.

4 The traditional quadruple is off the table for the Gunners Credit: Getty

A 2-0 loss in the Carabao Cup final to Manchester City on Sunday ensured Arsenal's wait for a first trophy since the FA Cup in 2020 would roll on.

However, unabashed Arsenal supporter Piers Morgan believes the quadruple is still on, but with a twist.



With the Gunners in the mix for the Premier League title, the FA Cup and the Champions League, how else can they complete the quadruple?

Well, according to Morgan, Arsenal can land a full house should Tottenham Hotspur endure the unthinkable and be relegated from the Premier League.

talkSPORT BET have even boosted the odds of that happening from 20/1 to 25/1.

Speaking on talkSPORT Breakfast, Morgan revealed how Arsenal supporters in the Bobby Moore Room at Wembley for the Carabao Cup final erupted with glee whenever Nottingham Forest netted against Tottenham on Sunday before he delivered a rousing rally cry in the hopes of inspiring relegation for the Gunners' local rivals.



Morgan delivers Churchillian address to spur on Gunners' quadruple

"Every time Forest scored, a massive cheer went up in the Bobby Moore Suite," Morgan said.

"I don't think I've ever seen Arsenal fans cheering a rival team's goals more than we did.



"When they won 3-0, there was almost a standing ovation in the Bobby Moore Suite.

"Obviously, my dream of the quintuple has sadly crumbled. We can now only win the quadruple: Champions League, Premier League, FA Cup and Spurs going down. That is still on, Arsenal fans.



"So let us rally together, let's rally, go forward and win.

4 Morgan isn't far away from his dream of Spurs being relegated coming true Credit: Getty

"Remember the words of Winston Churchill, let us go forward together. Never give in, we will prevail!"

How Arsenal are still on for the treble

Of those four scenarios, Arsenal winning the Premier League appears the likeliest.

Mikel Arteta's side sit nine points clear at the top of the table ahead of second-placed Manchester City.

However, the gap could be slashed to just six points with seven games remaining should City win their game in hand.



The Gunners are seeking their first Premier League title since the iconic 'Invincibles' campaign from 2003/04.

In the FA Cup, Arsenal are in the quarter-finals and will face Championship side Southampton for a spot in the final four.

4 Arteta is hoping to help Arsenal shake off their tag as perennial bridesmaids Credit: Getty

By simply making the final eight, it is Arsenal's best run in the FA Cup since they won it at the end of the 2019/20 campaign.

In the seasons following their last piece of silverware, Arteta's side has never made it beyond the fourth round in the leading domestic competition.



Arsenal are also in the last eight of the Champions League, with a two-legged tie against Portuguese side Sporting to come.

The Gunners are yet to win a major European trophy, although they came close to ending the drought in 2019 when they finished runners-up in the Europa League to Chelsea.

Should Arsenal beat Sporting to reach the Champions League semi-finals for a second season running, a clash against Barcelona or Atletico Madrid would await.

However, the joy derived from winning one, two or indeed all three trophies still up for grabs may not surpass the pleasure Arsenal fans would take in seeing Tottenham get relegated.

4 Tottenham's stay in the Premier League could be coming to an embarrassing end Credit: Getty



Spurs last endured the ignominy of relegation in 1977, but should lightning strike twice nearly 50 years later, it would be far more humiliating.

They couldn't... could they?

Igor Tudor's side sit 17th in the league with 30 points, one above 18th-placed West Ham United.

The remaining fixtures for Tottenham are not kind, either.

Of the club's last seven league matches this term, three are against teams in the top eight who are chasing European football.

Tottenham must also face Leeds United, who are one of their rivals in the battle for Premier League survival.

It's highly unlikely Arsenal supporters will get to enjoy the dream scenario of all four eventualities becoming reality.



But, with Morgan's version of a quadruple very much alive, perhaps Gunners supporters can afford to fantasise about what could be.

Football.title: Memphis Depay forced to release statement after being told to stop using phone on bench
Football.content:
Memphis Depay forced to release statement after being told to stop using phone on bench

Memphis Depay has revealed the reason he was using his phone whilst sat on the bench for Corinthians.

The Dutch forward appeared to be told off by a member of the club's coaching staff during Sunday's draw with Flamengo.

3 Depay has released a statement after using his phone whilst on the bench for Corinthians Credit: x.com/topskillsportuk

Depay had been spotted on his phone as he sat alongside his teammates during stoppage time at the end of the second half.



The 32-year-old had been forced off injured just 22 minutes into the Brazilian Serie A match.

As the match approached it's conclusion, television cameras picked up on Depay using his phone before being approached by a Corinthians coach.

The staff member appeared to tell him to stop as they exchanged words, with Depay seen putting his phone back into his pocket.

Depay statement

He has now responded to the incident in a statement shared to social media.



Writing on X, Depay explained: "Just to clarify, my moment with the phone was purely to communicate with the medical staff in the Netherlands at that moment.

"I came outside to show support to my team while I could’ve stayed inside the dressing room with the injury.



"I’m upset with the result of the game as well. We keep working for better days."

Depay had been included in the latest Netherlands squad on Friday for their international friendlies against Norway and Ecuador.



The severity of his injury remains unknown, with the former Manchester United man yet to withdraw from the squad.

3 Depay appeared to be told off by a member of coaching staff for his antics Credit: x.com/topskillsportuk

Meanwhile, his club side went on to draw 1-1 at home to their state rivals.

Lucas Paqueta had given Flamengo the lead before Yuri Alberto equalised for the home side.

Corinthians were unable to find a winner despite their opponents being reduced to 10 men in the second half.



The result leaves them 11th in the table after eight matches, although Flamengo are just four points ahead in fourth.

Depay joined Corinthians in September 2024 after leaving Atletico Madrid two months prior.

During his time in Brazil, he has scored 20 goals and added 15 assists in 77 appearances across all competitions.

3 Depay explained he had been speaking with Dutch medical staff after he was forced off Credit: Getty

He has also won three trophies including the Brazilian Cup and Sao Paulo State Championship.



Depay is currently out of contract in the summer having signed a two-year deal upon joining the club.

News.title: Moment cowering burglar is found hiding underneath a bed as he’s arrested after breaking into home
News.content:
THIS is the moment a cowering burglar is caught hiding under a bed after breaking into a home.

Body worn footage shows officers lifting up a bed frame and finding the suspect squashed inside the storage compartment.





David Dale Thompson can be seen curled up in the cramped space as officers drag him out from his hiding spot and arrest him.

Thompson, 42, of no fixed abode, was caught on January 15, 2025 after being tracked down by cops on patrol.

He’s now been caged for five years after pleading guilty to four counts of residential burglary.

Manchester Crown Court heard how police had been stepping up patrols in Rusholme and Moss Side.

The increased police presence follows a spike in break-ins in those areas.

Moments before officers spotted Thompson acting suspiciously while riding a bike at around 5pm.

They followed him to a nearby property on Boscombe Street.



Cops became suspicious and moved in and forced entry to the address.

But it was inside one room where the dramatic moment unfolded.

After lifting the bed, officers discovered Thompson hiding inside beneath.

Detective Inspector Natalie McDonald, of GMP’s Manchester Central Neighbourhood Crime Team, commended the work of the officers.

She said: “This was a fantastic example of proactive policing, which has resulted in a serial offender now behind bars.



“We know how devastating a burglary in your own home can be and this shows our commitment to tackling them head on.

“If you have any information or concerns about neighbourhood crime in your area, make sure you speak to us.

“You can do this by calling 101 or reporting issues on http://www.gmp.police.uk, in the meantime we have officers on regular patrols in areas that need us the most.”

News.title: My daughter was stabbed 20 times in her bed by flatmate who bombarded her with 40,000 messages & spied on her having sex
News.content:
DEADLY OBSESSION My daughter was stabbed 20 times in her bed by flatmate who bombarded her with 40,000 messages & spied on her having sex

STEPHANIE Hansen was one of life’s carers – so when she noticed a colleague sitting alone every day in the work lunch hall, she felt sorry for him and started a conversation.

“She was a friend to everyone and anyone who knew her, loved her,” says her mum. Glenda.





The quiet, loner diner, Sheldon Rodrigues, was delighted to have met someone to talk to in the canteen at Heathrow Airport, where they both worked.

And when he later suggested that, instead of them both renting small houses in Hayes, West London, they share a bigger one together, with a bedroom each and a garden, Stephanie agreed.

As a housemate, Rodrigues became increasingly possessive of her, wanting to know where she was and what she was doing every day. Stephanie found it irritating but told her family and friends he was harmless.



But after she started a relationship with another work colleague, Rodrigues became insanely jealous and bombarded her daily with hundreds of WhatsApp messages, declaring his love and warning her to stay away from her boyfriend.

Unnervingly, even when Rodrigues was away, he knew when her boyfriend was visiting for, unbeknown to Stephanie, he heard every conversation via bugging devices planted in the house. He was even able to listen in to them having sex.

In a rage, he returned home from his night shift in the early hours of 30 December, 2022, after her boyfriend had left, and savagely beat and stabbed Stephanie, 39, to death in bed, where she had been peacefully sleeping.

In the documentary, WhatsApp Obsession: The Murder of Stephanie Hansen, her family, as well as investigative police officers, talk about how Stephanie’s friendly nature and Rodrigues’ infatuation for her was a recipe for murder.



After Rodrigues dialled 999 to report having found his flatmate dead, the police arrived and were horrified by what they saw.

DCI Neil John, Metropolitan Police, recalls: “It was horrific. There were 20 stab wounds and 39 blunt force trauma injuries which were all centred around her head, face and neck. Blood was everywhere.

“We didn’t find the knife that caused the stab wounds but there were blooded fingerprints on a hairdryer and a tower fan in her bedroom which had been used to strike her with.”

Rodrigues, 30, was quick to blame the murder on Stephanie’s new boyfriend, Celso, a married father-of-three, who she had recently begun dating, telling detectives: “Her boyfriend stayed here two nights ago, when I was at work, on my night shift. He was gone before I finished my shift.”



“When the police told me she had been found dead, I was totally confused,” says Glenda. “I couldn’t understand what happened or why.”

Stephanie’s brother, Scott, was equally mystified. “She didn’t hang around with violent people, drugs dealers or criminals,” he says. “There was no one we knew who would do something like this to her. Everyone she knew, loved her.”

In the bedroom, police found an open packet of wound dressings that had been bought from Boots the chemist. When they asked Rodrigues about it, he said that on Christmas Day he had cut his hand while carving the chicken and showed them his palm, which he had patched up with the dressings.

It seemed dubious, as did his narrative of continuing to point the police to Stephanie’s boyfriend and so they arrested Rodrigues on suspicion of murder.

The family were startled when they were told. “I remember years ago, Stephanie saying, ‘There’s this chap at work and he just sits in the lunch hall and he’s on his own,’” says Glenda. “She met him about 2015 and she just felt sorry for him.



“I also remember that her phone would be pinging and pinging and I’d say, ‘Who on earth is that?’ And she would reply, ‘Oh, it’s just him. Ignore it.’ She looked after him and he obviously took a shine to her.”

While being held at the police station, the investigation revealed Rodrigues’ jealous obsession with Stephanie in a lengthy series of WhatsApp messages, declaring his undying love.

She would repeatedly tell him to stop and that she wanted them just to be friends, but he continued to bombard her.

“There were in excess of 40,000 WhatsApp messages, going back to 2015,” says DS Brian Jones. “It was endless and it reads like a storyboard, ultimately.”



During his police interview, he told police that Stephanie’s boyfriend had threatened her and said, “If I can’t have you, then I won’t let you be with anyone else.”

“The boyfriend was located and volunteered his DNA and fingerprints and gave us his mobile phone,” says Brian. “Everywhere he said that he went on the day Stephanie was murdered, was the case. He was telling the truth.

“He mentioned that Sheldon was possessive and very jealous and that he had a thing for her.”

Celso added that once, when Rodrigues was on holiday in his home state of Goa, India, he had been at the house with Stephanie when she began to get a series of texts from him, outraged that she had a man there.

Stephanie had said she thought he was spying on her.



A more intense police search of the house turned up several spying devices. Some were disguised as plug adaptors which, when linked to Rodrigues’s handset, would phone him when they detected a noise, enabling him to hear what was going on in the house.

In his work fleece in the hallway, they found a USB plug that had a spy camera in the end of it.

Rodrigues told police he had bought them for security purposes but he had never used them.



As his jealousy grew, so did the volume and intensity of his text messages to Stephanie – “You’re playing with fire here,” “Whatever you’re up to I don’t want problems coming into the house,” “I’m not going to let it end like this,” “Nobody can come between us.”

But there were also some exchanges that indicated they had once had something together. When Sheldon wrote, “Can we just do it 1 more time? That’s all I’m asking for,” she replied, “No, you just need to back off and be my friend.”

Her friend, Lucy, confirmed that Stephanie had once returned home drunk, from a work Christmas outing and slept with him

“She said it was the worst mistake she had ever made,” says Lucy.

“I think that’s what pushed him over the edge,” says Scott. “He was attached to her from that moment on and, unfortunately, some men are like that.”



On 29 December, the night before she was murdered, Celso came round at 8.30pm while Rodrigues was at work.

“The data on Rodrigues’ handset paints a very clear picture of what he’s been doing through his nightshift,” says Brian. “He’s effectively listening to their conversation, listening to them having sex.

He texted her, saying that he might come back for a smoke. He knows that Celso is there and that Stephanie is going to be panicked by that message. I think it tipped him over the edge and he came home after work in a rage.”

In building their case against Rodrigues, police found CCTV footage that showed him buying the wound dressing at Boots on 30 December at 5.12pm before he returned home, ordered food, and made the 999 call, the following morning.

CCTV footage in his workplace showed other crucial evidence of his lies where he is seen high-fiving a colleague, earlier that same day, using the hand that he said he had cut on Christmas Day. But here, there is clearly no injury to his hand.



Stephanie had been wearing just a pyjama top when she was found dead and on her legs were small, circular drops of blood that looked like they had fallen there from a height. They were most likely to belong to her attacker. The DNA proved to be that of Rodrigues’.

The blood on the fan and hair dryer was Stephanie’s but the fingerprints, Rodrigues’.

“A number of forensic swabs were taken from Stephanie for analysis and DNA belonging to Rodrigues, was found on her stomach,” says Neil. “It’s an unthinkable scenario that something of a sexual nature may have happened pre or post murder. We simply don’t know.”



On 23 January, 2024, he was found guilty of murder and received a minimum 25-year prison sentence.

“He kept his head down throughout his time in court and showed no emotion,” says Scott. “But we were delighted with the length of the sentence.”

All of the thousands of WhatsApp messages were read out in court, showing how his infatuation and desperation grew.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“They showed that he was completely obsessed with her,” says Glenda. “It was only at trial that I realised that she was being stalked. There was this coercive control that she didn’t realise he was doing, because she was in control of her life… until she wasn’t.”

WhatsApp Obsession: The Murder of Stephanie Hansen, airs on Tuesday on ITV1.

News.title: Iran secretly agrees to give up nuke plans, Trump says after talks avert huge blitz – but regime DENIES negotiations
News.content:
DONALD Trump made the bombshell claim that Iran has secretly agreed to give up its nuclear plans – as the reeling regime denies that talks have taken place.

The US president earlier called off a major blitz of Iran’s energy network after holding “very good and productive conversations” with Tehran.





9 Credit: truthsocial

Trump had vowed to “decimate” the country’s power plants if they did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by midnight.

Speaking to reporters outside Air Force One, Trump added that in order for talks to be successful, Iran would need to have “no more nuclear weapons”.

“That’s point number one, two and three,” he said

“They will never have a nuclear weapon.”

Asked whether Iran would relinquish its nuclear ambitions, Trump replied: “They’ve agreed to that.”

It comes as…



But Iran’s Fars news agency has flatly denied that direct talks had occurred between the warring nations.

It has also vowed to continue wreaking havoc on the global economy with its blockade of the strait.

Fuel prices have rocketed from under $73 (£55) a barrel on the eve of the conflict to $113 (£85), sparking fears of a worldwide recession.

Tehran accused Trump of backing down only after the murderous regime threatened to blitz neighbouring countries hosting US bases.

Trump later rebuked Iran’s claim, telling reporters: “They called, I didn’t call. They want to make a deal.”



And he suggested that it had been Iran who capitulated out of fear of US strikes.

“Tomorrow morning, sometime their time, we were expecting to blow up their largest electric generating plants that cost over 10 billion US dollars to build.

“It was a very good one, there was no dearth of money. One shot it is gone. It collapses. Why would they want that?

“So they called, I didn’t call. They called. They want to make a deal.



Trump said he had ordered the Department of War to pause any planned strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for five days, while negotiations continue and depending on their success.

Downing Street praised Trump’s announcement, with a No10 spokesperson saying: “Any reports of productive talks are welcome.

“We have always said that a swift resolution to the war is in global interests and the Strait of Hormuz specifically needs to be reopened.”

They also shed some light on the phone call between Sir Keir Starmer and the US president on Sunday, explaining that the pair specifically discussed the Strait of Hormuz.



Despite Trump’s declaration that he would postpone attacks on energy facilities, Israel announced a fresh wave of strikes in Tehran on Monday.

Trump had issued the 48-hour ultimatum on Saturday and doubled down on the threat in a fiery interview, saying: “You’ll find out what’s gonna happen. You’re gonna find out soon.

“Total decimation of Iran. It’s gonna work out very good.”

Iran’s regime hit back with chilling threats of its own, warning that US bases and energy sites across the Middle East would become targets if America struck.



Power plants in Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, Iraq, Syria and Turkey were all named in the firing line.

Lieutenant Colonel Ebrahim Zolfaqari said: “Everything is ready for a major jihad aimed at the total destruction of US economic interests in West Asia.

“We did not start this war and will not initiate it now, but if the enemy harms our power plants, we will do whatever is necessary to defend our country and national interests.”

Tensions had already exploded after Iran fired missiles towards the US-UK base on Diego Garcia in a major escalation that stunned military chiefs.

One missile failed, while another was shot down by a US warship – but the attack raised fears Tehran can now strike targets up to 4,000km away.



The twisted regime had also issued a chilling warning that even tourist hotspots could be hit.

IRGC General Abolfazl Shekarchi said: “From now on… even parks, recreational areas, and tourist destinations anywhere in the world will no longer be safe for you.”

Meanwhile, the US had been gearing up for a major offensive.

Around 2,500 Marines were deployed to the region, while Navy SEALs, submarines and stealth helicopters were put on standby for a high-risk mission.

Plans were even being drawn up to seize Kharg Island – Iran’s key oil export hub – in a move designed to choke off the regime’s finances.



Military chiefs have insisted Iran was already faltering.

Admiral Brad Cooper, commander of US Central Command, said: “They’re operating in a sign of desperation.

“In the last couple of weeks, they’ve attacked civilian targets very deliberately, more than 300 times.”



RECOMMENDED STORIES

He added Iran’s firepower was dwindling: “At the beginning of the conflict, you saw large volumes in the dozens of drones and missiles.

“You no longer see that. It’s all one or two at a time.”

News.title: Teen farm worker crushed to death after getting trapped inside hay bale machine, inquest hears
News.content:
FARM TRAGEDY Teen farm worker crushed to death after getting trapped inside hay bale machine, inquest hears

A TEENAGE farm worker was crushed to death in a bale wrapping machine – as his older brother worked in an adjacent field, an inquest heard.

Luke Searle, 17, had been operating a tractor and baling machine to wrap up cut grass bales used for animal feed.



But an inquest heard that there was a problem with the bale wrapper and Luke went to investigate but became caught in the machine.



The inquest at Exeter Coroner’s Court heard that farm owner James Williams went to check on Luke and found the machinery was not moving in the field.

He discovered Luke trapped and unconscious in the baler.

Mr Williams, who operates a partnership including five farms and 2,000 acres, called for help which included Luke’s older brother William racing to the field.

They freed Luke from the machine, gave him CPR but he was pronounced dead at the scene.

The inquest heard that Luke’s family and the farm owners, the Williams, had been close friends for many years.

Luke was working at Sherwell Farm near Plympton, Devon, in June 2022 when the tragic incident happened at around 8pm one evening.

He was a student at Cornwall based Duchy College on a two year agricultural machinery land-based engineering course with 80% of the course being on the farm.

Health and Safety Executive inspector Simon Jones told the hearing there had been a problem with the bale wrapper arms not rotating and Luke went to investigate the fault.



But as he did so the arms started again in an “unexpected and unplanned movement” trapping him and crushing him.

He had not put the machinery into a safe mode before investigating the fault, the inquest was told.

The jury heard the power should be isolated, the engine switched off and controls put in neutral before any repair.

Nicholas Lane, the Area Coroner, told the inquest jury that the Searle brothers were very close and had a rural upbringing and planned careers in farming.

They heard Luke was “a very sociable well liked young man who loved what he was doing”.



The inquest continues and will last several days.

News.title: Dealer & YouTube gangster dubbed ‘The General’ who UK can’t deport because no country will take him is arrested AGAIN
News.content:
BUSTED AGAIN Dealer & YouTube gangster dubbed ‘The General’ who UK can’t deport because no country will take him is arrested AGAIN

A NOTORIOUS gang leader known as The General has been arrested again – after the UK failed to deport him as no country will take him.

Joland Giwa has boasted about his crimes as a member of the Croydon-based crime ring Don’t Say Nothing (DSN) on YouTube for years.





The 36-year-old arrived in the UK when he was just 10 and he is believed to be from either Nigeria or Sierra Leone.

But, the Home Office has been unable to deport the criminal because neither country will take him.

Officials instead decided that Giwa would be sent to Newport, in Wales, to cut him off from his criminal connections in London.



Giwa was sentenced to 27 months in February 2009 after committing a string of robberies and thefts.

He ended up living in a bail hostel in Newport after serving his time.

The YouTuber found himself locked up again in 2015 for a terrifying robbery at a William Hill bookies in Ringland.

The late former Labour MP Paul Flynn said at the time: “There was outrage when a man described by a court and the police as so “dangerous” they didn’t want him in London was sent to Newport.



“Since coming to this city he has committed [a] violent robbery.

“Newport has a long tradition of proud achievement in assimilating immigrants but if you start putting excessive numbers in a place you get excessive pressures.

“There ought to be a more even spread across the country.”

Giwa was nicked once again in 2020 for trafficking drugs into Newport and was jailed for four years.

Officers had caught the gangster selling drugs at a children’s play park after arriving on an electric scooter.



The court heard Giwa was caught with class A drugs with a potential street value of £4,700, £1,300 in cash and three mobile phones.

The defendant admitted possession with intent to supply heroin, possession with intent to supply crack cocaine and possession with intent to supply cocaine.

Giwa has now been busted again for the same offences, which took place on February 17 this year.

The defendant found himself back in Cardiff Crown Court and pleaded guilty.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Giwa will be sentenced on April 10 and he has been remanded in custody.



The Home Office has been asked to comment.

News.title: Russian TV airs bizarre propaganda song about how great life is without internet as Putin’s blackout enters 3rd week
News.content:
RUSSIAN state TV has broadcast a bizarre propaganda song about how good life is without internet amid Vladimir Putin’s sweeping blackout.

Strange footage showed a group of children chanting “we don’t want internet” as the Kremlin‘s online disruption enters its third week.





Russians in major cities including Moscow and St Petersburg have faced unprecedented network outages over the last month.

With Putin being hit with accusations of plotting a massive online clampdown, Russian state TV has aired footage of a children’s group praising the benefits of having no internet in a mind-boggling song.

The propaganda piece suggests that without internet access “there are no blogs, no channels,” allowing children to spend more time playing “badminton”.

The lyrics also claim that “phones are unnecessary” and praise the fact that “blue light of the screen” no longer interferes with meals.



The song also calls on children to stop worrying about missed lessons or disruption to their daily lives.

Another line in the chant says: “You won’t get hooked – even if the lesson isn’t learned, it’s more fun to see friends in real life.”

Mysterious network blackouts have plagued Russian cities after Putin signed a law requiring telecom operators to shut off internet access whenever the Kremlin says so.

Shortly after the law took effect on March 3, residents across the capital reported widespread issues with internet access and communications.



With Russians left in the dark amid the internet blackout, fears have mounted that Moscow is planning a huge digital crackdown.

A huge surge in internet outages was also reported on Sunday in St Petersburg – which also came amid flight chaos due to a Ukrainian drone strike near Russia‘s second largest city.

Russians have also taken to social media to make light of being plunged into the dark ages.

Residents uploaded clips of themselves using their mobile phones to cut vegetables and play table tennis.

Footage shared on social media also showed Muscovites unfolding massive maps to work out their commute in true Eastern European humor.



Comedian Andrei Kaygorodov shared a skit on Instagram, titled “How to use your mobile phone in the centre of Moscow.”

He is then seen using it as a table tennis racket, instead of a knife to chop a cucumber and as a trouser strap.

Another one posted on Tuesday, titled “Residents when they find out that the internet is working in Moscow,” sees him popping a Champagne bottle.



“And they say that Russians never smile,” he jokes in the caption.

The disruptions are clearly affecting daily life as pro-government outlet Moscow-24 reported a surge in demand for alternative communication devices.

Sales of printed atlases and travel guides have also increased.

Between March 6 and March 10, purchases reportedly skyrocketed by 48 per cent compared with the previous week.

Russia has long censored digital platforms such as Facebook and Instagram.



But the recent surge in internet restrictions has caused panic-buying and fears of a larger comprehensive crackdown on dissent in Russia.

It has sparked widespread concern in Moscow – Russia’s capital of 13 million people that has been relatively unscathed by the four-year war in Ukraine.

The blackout also came as a popular pro-Putin blogger suddenly turned on the Russian tyrant.



In an unprecedented move, Ilya Remeslo, 42, delivered a scathing criticism of the despot – demanding that Putin be put on trial as a war criminal.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The Z-blogger called for Putin’s resignation and the toppling of his regime.

The internet outages have also raised rumours that there is an imminent coup in Russia – with the authorities trying to stamp out the potential threat.

News.title: Webcam sex star, 29, who fled to UK and ‘abandoned daughter, 2, when she went clubbing’ loses bid to block extradition
News.content:
MONSTER MUM Webcam sex star, 29, who fled to UK and ‘abandoned daughter, 2, when she went clubbing’ loses bid to block extradition

A FUGITIVE webcam model who fled to Britain after abandoning her toddler to go clubbing has lost her bid to block extradition.

Cristina Târnoveanu, 29, is wanted by Romanian authorities and has been ordered to return to her home country to serve a four and half year prison sentence.





She fled Romania after leaving her two-year-old daughter home alone without food in squalid conditions while she went on nights out in the city of Iasi.

The online adult performer was jailed for four years and six months in March last year for the ill-treatment of a minor.

But she left her home country after being released pending appeal and was later tracked down 1,300 miles away – in Doncaster, South Yorkshire.

Her disappearance sparked fury in Romania, where she was placed on a most-wanted list.

Cristina last week lost her fight against being sent back from Britain and is now set to be returned to Romania to serve her sentence.

Westminster Magistrates’ Court confirmed she had been “ordered to Romania on conditional bail”.

Romanian prosecutors said the youngster was left alone on at least three occasions between June and August 2019 in Iasi, with one incident lasting more than eight hours.



The tot was eventually found after a neighbour heard her crying and alerted police.

She was taken into care the next day.

Prosecutors said: “While she was away from home, whether at work, for personal reasons, for leisure purposes or in nightclubs, the defendant left her daughter, aged two and a half, unsupervised, locked inside the property and in improper living conditions.

“Namely in an environment with waste and faecal matter, without providing the necessary food for the period in which the child was left alone.”

Cristina was arrested by officers from the National Extradition Unit last August after Romanian authorities demanded her return.



She was found living in a first-floor flat on one of Doncaster’s main shopping streets.

When previously approached by The Sun, Cristina – who was under curfew and wearing an electronic tag – claimed Romanian reports about the case were “wrong”.

She denied fleeing to the UK, but refused to explain when she arrived in Britain.



She confirmed: “I have refused extradition and I am fighting them back.”

Cristina can lodge an appeal against extradition at the High Court.

News.title: We’ve been forced to rip down four ‘ugly’ horse statues – our neighbours absolutely hate them… but they’re just jealous
News.content:
TRAVELLERS who crowned their home with “ugly” horse statues have blasted their neighbours after losing a bitter planning row.

Brandon Rawlings, 27, and his wife Paige, 25, insist the complaints over their decorations were driven by those who “don’t like Gypsies” and want the family “kicked out”.





But furious locals have hit back by branding the couple “neighbours from hell” – and claiming the horse-topped wall is only one part of a wider catalogue of alleged disruption in the quiet village street in Hampshire.

Mr Rawlings, who is currently in prison, was ordered by council planners to demolish the bold boundary, where brick pillars topped with ornaments of rearing horses stand 2.6 metres tall.

Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council launched enforcement action after ruling the works were carried out without permission and were harmful to the character of the street.



Planning papers reveal how Mr Rawlings fought back against the council – claiming his family were being victimised by local residents.

He claimed to have been told “our kind isn’t welcome here”, adding: “I do feel like we are being victimised because we are Romany Gypsies and I feel the street are out to just cause us problems.”

In an appeal, Mr Rawlings insisted he had improved the look of his address “100 times”, claiming: “We as a family feel like we are targeted because we are Romany Gypsies and that is why we are getting all this bother.

“Our home doesn’t affect the character of the street, it improves the look of the street rather than the overgrown hedges and untidy drive/gardens. Ours is tidy and I think a lot of this comes down to jealousy!’



He added: “I think this all boils down to racism because we have horses on top and the street doesn’t like gypsies. Everybody that sees this place says how we have improved it.

“The only people who moan are the ones who own their house and have told us to our face our kind isn’t welcome here.”

Neighbours painted a very different picture, accusing the couple of making life a “nightmare” for those living nearby.

One neighbour, who asked not to be named, said: “They are neighbours from hell. They do whatever they want and get away with it. It’s appalling. I have had enough, we all have.

“They are a nightmare. I cannot tolerate it anymore. They are a law upon themselves.



“They have breached planning regulations with the horses but nobody has done anything. They just get away with it.

“The horses themselves are ugly. All the work they have done is terrible.

Another neighbour added: “What they’ve done to that house is bad enough. The front looks like a prison compound.



“They’re absolutely neighbours from hell. I would not wish them to live next to my worst enemy.”

Mr Rawlings is currently “in prison”, according to wife Paige, who defended the family, saying: “We are being victimised. We are good neighbours. We are kind to people.

“We were told to remove the trees. We really like the statues. We won’t be taking them down. People just want us to get kicked out.

“They report us. It’s not fair. We could make it look all scruffy, but we’ve looked after it.”

Mr Rawlings wall makeover was completed in late 2021 and he later applied unsuccessfully for retrospective planning permission.



Council planners said the boundary was out of keeping with the surrounding street, where most homes have low hedges or simple fences along the front.

They also warned the couple’s new gates were not set back far enough, meaning drivers would have to stop on the road while opening them.

Two objections were made by local residents, according to planning papers. One said: “Brick wall of that height with horses [is] completely out of character for the road.”

Mr Rawlings insisted the boundary was built to blend in with nearby hedges and stop his children from running into the busy road.

He said: “The fences we have are four foot with a trellis that you can see through.



“I have a massive dog so three foot fences would be no good and wouldn’t be secure for her or my children.

“My driveway needs gates to keep my children safe. My property does not affect the street view at all.’

Mr Rawlings then appealed to the independent Planning Inspectorate but the challenge was rejected this month.

In the ruling, inspector Shaun Harrington said the wall and statues appear an “obvious, jarring urban feature” that clashed with the area’s hedge-lined streets.

He said Mr Rawlings’ concerns about discrimination and the safety of his children had been considered.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

However, Mr Harrington concluded there was no compelling evidence that such a tall structure was necessary.

An enforcement notice was upheld ordering the structures to be removed – or reduced to no more than one metre in height – within six months.

News.title: 'I went to meet Sarah Ferguson but what Princess Beatrice did stunned me'
News.content:
'I went to meet Sarah Ferguson but what Princess Beatrice did stunned me'

A report who arranged a meeting with Sarah Ferguson was taken aback to find her daughter Princess Beatrice also in attendance - and she found the way the princess conducted herself "fascinating"

View 3 Images Princess Beatrice (left) attended a meeting her mother Sarah Ferguson (centre) arranged (Image: Getty Images Europe)

A journalist who went to visit Sarah Ferguson was surprised to find Princess Beatrice also present at the meeting - and her conduct left the reporter stunned.

The former Duchess of York and her daughters, along with Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, have found themselves mired in controversy in recent months after all were the subject of multiple mentions in the Epstein Files. There is no suggestion of wrongdoing by Beatrice, her sister Eugenie and Sarah while Andrew denies wrongdoing in relation to their connection with Epstein.

The controversy surrounding their parents has placed Beatrice and Eugenie in a difficult position, and now people who have had first-hand experience of the York family have being father their say on their conduct.

The Times' royal editor Kate Mansey went to meet Sarah to discuss the possibility of doing an interview , but said "to my great surprise, Princess Beatrice was there".

But it was Beatrice's role in the meeting that really left the journalist stunned, as she reportedly analysed everything her mother said before clarifying her comments.

View 3 Images Princess Beatrice (left) left a journalist stunned (Image: Dave Benett/Getty Images for The Anti Slavery Collective)

Speaking on the Times Royal podcast, Kate said: "She sat and she took notes. I thought it was fascinating how she interacted, she's super smart, she was kind of analysing everything Sarah Ferguson was saying and kind of clarifying.

"Fergie was like: 'I want to come across as this, and I'd like to be known as this, and if we do it I want it to be done like this, and I want you to talk about my work over here with this charity.'

"I was taking it all in, she's hitting me with this barrage of thoughts and ideas, and Beatrice was to the side.

"She was analysing. 'You know just to clarify what mummy's saying....' She was very organised, she was very thoughtful and processing her mother's thoughts in real-time and acting as a conduit."

There has been a great deal of talk about Beatrice and Eugenie's place in the royal pecking order in light of the scandal engulfing their parents.

This includes calls for them to be removed from the line of succession along with hints that they won't be showing their faces at royal events where they would normally have been welcome, like Royal Ascot.

View 3 Images Princess Beatrice, Sarah Ferguson and Princess Eugenie (Image: Getty Images)

According to Kate, her anecdote is now "interesting" due to the accusations of misconduct in a public office against her father Andrew.

She said: "That is interesting now of course because you wonder how close she was to any of the business deals that Sarah Ferguson or perhaps her father was doing at the time.

Article continues below

"Now we know from people who worked on that infamous Newsnight interview that the talks about talks, as journalists often call them, where you have the preliminary chats about the possibility of doing an interview.

"There's talks about talks that happened at the Palace with Andrew, with Amanda Thirsk, his then-private secretary, and the Newsnight team, Beatrice was there taking notes."

Andrew was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in a public office last month. He was later released under investigation.

News.title: UK snow maps show England and Wales hit for 48 hours as 25 counties on alert
News.content:
UK snow maps show England and Wales hit for 48 hours as 25 counties on alert

Snow is forecast to fall across the UK in less than two days, with several areas, including Manchester, Liverpool and Belfast in the firing line - as the Met Office warned it would turn 'markedly colder'

View 2 Images Snow could cover multiple UK cities by Wednesday (Image: Getty)

The UK is bracing for a 48-hour window of snow that could see up to 25 counties hit, new weather charts show.

‌



Snow is set to land across multiple towns and cities in less than two days, with as much as 8cm falling in parts of Scotland, 6cm in Wales and 3cm landing in cities across England by Wednesday, March 25, according to data by WXCharts. Several larger cities look set to be in the firing line, including Manchester, Liverpool, Blackpool, and Belfast.

‌



The most intense snowfall is forecast for areas on higher ground, with hills in the south of Powys, Wales, set for up to 6cm by 9am. The forecast comes as the Met Office warned the UK would be "turning markedly colder from Wednesday."

‌



Wednesday will also bring some sunny spells and blustery showers, with snow over northern hills, the forecaster said.

View 2 Images A map shows snow landing in multiple areas this week

"By Wednesday, it will feel markedly colder across all parts, with rain, sleet and hail showers potentially quite widely, and snow likely over higher ground in the north," the Met Office said.

‌



"Elsewhere, it will be a blustery day and although there is some sunshine on offer, it will feel cold thanks to the wind chill. Temperatures will fall overnight - potentially to -5°C in rural Scotland - with a widespread frost overnight likely for most, and ice at first in the north on Thursday morning.

"Most places remain dry with sunny spells on Thursday, although cloud and rain pushes into Northern Ireland and western parts later on. Temperatures return closer to average from Friday onwards, though further weather systems may affect the UK at the weekend, most likely affecting northern areas".

Full list of UK counties set for snow this week

Here are the counties where snow is forecast between 3am on Wednesday, March 25, and Thursday, March 26, at 6am, according to WXCharts.

‌

News.title: 'Black widow' carer stole £300k from OAP and spent it on Botox and lavish meals
News.content:
'Black widow' carer stole £300k from OAP and spent it on Botox and lavish meals

Pamela Gwinnett, 63, fled to Tenerife in breach of a court order under the guise of spreading her brother's ashes, and has been ordered to pay back money she stole from Joan Green

View 3 Images Pamela Gwinnett stole hundreds of thousands from a vulnerable elderly woman (Image: MEN MEDIA)

A carer dubbed the "Black Widow" who fleeced a pensioner of £300,000 spent her stolen fortune on Botox and lavish meals after fleeing the UK.

‌



Runaway fraudster Pamela Gwinnett isolated Joan Green, 89, from Chorley, Lancashire, from her family and plundered her cash while pretending to act as a carer during the Covid pandemic. Shameless Gwinnett, 63, a court heard before sentencing her to six years behind bars in absentia, got power of attorney over elderly Ms Green, changed her landline numbers and even sacked a long-term gardener to keep her isolated.

‌



She then stole from the mum, who died in 2022, using a joint bank account up to a total value of just over £296,000 while employed to carry out light cleaning and shopping tasks.

‌



Carer who fleeced £300k from pensioner then fled to Tenerife caught on video READ MORE :

View 3 Images Gwinnett has been told to pay back the money she stole from Ms Green

Sky News, which tracked Gwinnett to her new home in Tenerife, to where she fled after pocketing the six-figure sum, reports that she used the cash on cosmetic treatments, lavish meals, a car worth £22,500, and mortgages for properties she owned. She boldly claimed to the broadcaster that it was Ms Green's family who took her cash, despite a court finding her responsible.

Asked whether she had stolen the savings, she said: "No, never. Her family took it. I don't need to answer these questions. Did I heck take the money.

‌



"I was her power of attorney and executor, her solicitor's got all the money. I didn't have her bank card, her family had it. They have made me a scapegoat." Gwinnett, who denied her crimes before Preston Crown Court last year, was convicted by a jury of fraud by abuse of position and theft.

When Gwinnett fled to Tenerife, she did so in breach of a court order under the guise of scattering her dead brother's ashes, with Judge Michael Maher telling the court she has been there since April.

The judge said: “I know full well that you are in Tenerife and you have been there since April. Your application to vary your bail conditions to go to Tenerife, purportedly to scatter your brother’s ashes, was refused on April 2 2025. You ignored that court order and boarded a plane five hours later."

‌



View 3 Images Ms Green's family have said it is 'annoying' that Gwinnett is 'sunning it up' in Tenerife (Image: Lancs Live/MEN Media)

"I issued a bench warrant for your arrest on April 10 after you failed to attend on April 8 2025. You have been at large since that day. You have treated this process as you did Joan Green with utter contempt."

"From your remote location, you have continued to instruct your legal team." Gwinnett has made multiple attempts to appeal her six-year sentence, and Preston Crown Court last month made a confiscation order of £350,180.79 against her. She is ordered to pay the sum within three months, or face an additional three-and-a-half years tacked on to her existing sentence.

Article continues below

Gwinnett's crimes have now become known to other members of her flat complex, where neighbours have put up posters, some saying "wanted" and others saying: "Justice for Joan Green." Ms Green's family have been left furious that the woman who stole from her is "sunning it up in Tenerife".

David Bolton, her step-grandson, told Sky: "The fact [is] that she's just sunning it up in Tenerife, walking around, doesn't care - only about herself. All the pain she's caused everybody over here, all the upset. It's just so annoying."

He added that "evil" Gwinnett, is "very clever". Mr Bolton said: "She's evil but she's very, very clever at what she does. She knows how to play the system. This isn't her first time of doing it, there's no way. She's too confident, she's too clued up on what to do and how to behave."

News.title: Dr Amir Khan urges people to eat one food three times a day like he does
News.content:
Dr Amir Khan urges people to eat one food three times a day like he does

The health expert recommends eating the dried fruit daily

View 2 Images Three dates provide about 3g of fibre (stock image) (Image: Getty)

ITV's Dr Amir Khan is encouraging people to consider possibly following his lead and eating three dates a day. The health expert, who is a regular on TV programmes like This Morning and Lorraine, says that the fruit, which comes from date palm trees, is brimming with health benefits, and you only need as few as three per day.

‌



In an Instagram video, he outlined the benefits of incorporating this fruit into your diet. He said: "I have started eating three dates every day - people worry because they are sweet then they may affect blood sugars but some small studies suggest the opposite.

‌



"Here’s the study. They are only small studies and more research is needed on blood sugars but two to three dates can be eaten safely as part of a nutritionally balanced diet." According to the NHS, a portion of three dates counts as one of your daily servings of fruit and veg.

‌



Speaking to his followers, he added: "For the last year or so, I have taken to eating three dates a day. Dates don't negatively impact your long-term blood sugar, even if you're diabetic. Studies show that dates don't have an impact on average blood sugar levels or fasting sugars and actually some research shows that dates can lower your average blood sugars and even lower your cholesterol, that's probably because of their high-fibre content, and that high-fibre content makes them excellent for your gut health, too."

He also highlighted several other health benefits of dates, including helping combat harmful cells and reducing dementia risk. He added: "Now, the other health benefits of dates is that they are loaded with one of my favourite things, plant antioxidants and polyphenols, which can help reduce cell damage done by harmful free radicals that have been things like autoimmune conditions, cardiovascular disease and even some cancers.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



"Now early studies, and I have to stress these are just early studies, show that the antioxidants in dates can even improve brain health by reducing inflammation that has been linked to things like Alzheimer's disease, though we do need more research to support that. So if you're looking for an alternative sweet treat to refine sugar, you can't go wrong with dates."

Studies suggest that dates are nearly ideal foods thanks to their abundant essential nutrients and potential health benefits. Swapping your evening snacks for dates provides numerous benefits, as they're packed with dietary fibre and loaded with vitamins and minerals.

Dates are rich in fibre, which is a key nutrient for healthy bowel movements. In a 2015 study, people who ate seven dates each day for 21 days saw improved stool frequency and a notable increase in bowel movements compared to when they didn't eat dates.

‌



Dates contain powerful antioxidants like flavonoids, carotenoids and p henolic acid. F lavonoids can help reduce inflammation and studies show they may help lower the risk of diabetes, Alzheimer’s disease, and some types of cancer.

View 2 Images Dates are highly nutritious and fibre-rich

What are the health benefits of dates?

Low glycemic index

Lowers the risk of heart disease thanks to dietary fibres

Rich in antioxidants

Healthy digestion and promotes good gut bacteria because of high soluble and insoluble fibre

Nervous system support

Improved metabolism as they contain pantothenic acid, folate and niacin

Boosts energy

Regulates blood pressure due to potassium.

Prevents anaemia as they are high in iron

Strengthens bones due to significant amounts of minerals

Lowers cholesterol

Fights infections due to a high amount of sulfur

Boosts hair health as they contain vitamin B5

Article continues below

What should you know before eating dates?

Most nutritionists suggest consuming two to four dates per day as a nutritious portion containing beneficial nutrients as part of a well-balanced diet. However, the amount may vary between individuals depending on calorie requirements and existing health conditions. Consult your GP about how many times you should eat each day.

While dates are nutrient-dense, their high natural sugar content means moderation is key. It's sensible to adjust your date consumption based on your overall calorie requirements and other dietary choices, especially if you have health conditions such as type 2 diabetes. You might also consider pairing dates with a source of fat, protein, or fibre to moderate the blood sugar response.

For some people, excessive consumption of dates can lead to digestive issues, such as diarrhoea. Those with kidney disease need to exercise caution when eating dates. It's crucial to consult a doctor about the daily potassium intake.

News.title: Epping asylum hotel protestors plead guilty after brutal violent disorder
News.content:
Two men have admitted engaging in violent disorder following demonstrations outside a hotel housing asylum seekers in Essex.

Luke Fleming, 21, of Buckhurst Hill, and Shaun Thompson, 38, pleaded guilty at Chelmsford Crown Court on Monday to a charge of violent disorder relating to a protest outside The Bell Hotel in Epping on July 17 last year.

Thompson, of Epping, also pleaded guilty last month to a charge of criminal damage after being accused of punching a police car. Judge Alexander Mills released the defendants on bail and said a sentencing date would be confirmed in due course.

Multiple protests were held outside The Bell Hotel over the summer, after asylum seeker Hadush Gerberslasie Kebatu was charged with the sexual assault of a 14-year-old girl.

The Ethiopian national, who arrived in the UK on a small boat days before the incidents, was later found guilty of five offences – including sexual assault – at Chelmsford Magistrates’ Court.

Kebatu was jailed but wrongfully freed from HMP Chelmsford in October, before he was detained and deported to Ethiopia later that month.

News.title: Keir Starmer LIVE: PM grilled over Iran war handling after Donald Trump calls off attack
News.content:
Keir Starmer will be grilled by senior MPs on the Liaison Committee from 2:30pm.

Mr Starmer is expected to face questions on the conflict in Iran - and the impact on British families here at home as concerns grow over energy prices. After his interrogation, the Prime Minister will chair a meeting of the Government’s emergency Cobra committee as oil prices rose and markets slumped amid fears of escalation.

It comes after Donald Trump over the weekend threatened to obliterate Iran's power plants if the country did not reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz. No10 has since welcomed the US President's decision today to call off the immediate strikes.

The Liaison Committee is made up of chairs of the Commons' select committees. It questions the Prime Minister usually three times a year, normally in the final week become Parliament breaks up for recess. The Commons will rise on Thursday for Easter.

Follow live updates below...

News.title: Popular fashion retailer set to close 32 stores across the UK as iconic jewellers shut
News.content:
Popular fashion retailer set to close 32 stores across the UK as iconic jewellers shut

A number of stores are set to close in 2026, including popular fashion retailers and high street jewellery shops

View 3 Images A number of stores are set to close in 2026, including popular fashion retailers and high street jewellery shops (Image: William Barton via Getty Images)

UK fashion retailer River Island has shut down a total of 32 stores this year as part of a restructuring plan in the wake of a £32.3million pre-tax loss.

‌



The company's CEO, Ben Lewis, said that the firm has "a large portfolio of stores no longer aligned to our customers' needs".

‌



The rise of online shopping and escalating operating costs were cited as the primary challenges by the retailer. River Island, which has 200 outlets nationwide and employs over 5,000 people, continues to operate its remaining UK and Ireland branches after closing 32 sites, with no additional closures confirmed at present, reports the Express.

‌



View 3 Images The retailer is set to close 32 stores in 2026 (Image: Getty)

British jeweller Krafft Jewellers has announced the closure of its Chelmsford store in Essex, after half a century of trading. This was the only physical shop operated by the jewellers.

‌



In an official statement, the company said: "To all of our loyal and valued customers, I regret to inform you that after over 50 years of trading, Krafft Jewellers will be closing on March 31, 2026. This decision has not been easy, but the trading climate has become so much harder over the past few years.

"Unfortunately, the advance in online shopping and increasing costs has impacted so many high street businesses, ours included."

View 3 Images Family-run jewellery chain C W Sellors recently collapsed into administration (Image: Getty)

‌



Another family-owned jewellery chain, C W Sellors, has been rescued after falling into administration earlier this year, safeguarding 46 jobs. The high street jeweller has recently been snapped up by a fresh consortium of owners, comprising members of the firm's leadership team and external investors.

C W Sellors, which has been in business since 1979, operates ten stores across the UK, including locations in Whitby, York, Ashbourne, Bakewell, Matlock and Shrewsbury. Lee Causer, from business advisory firm BDO, stated earlier this year: "The business has been experiencing challenging trading conditions, with rising overheads and lower demand for its high-end products."

Chief executive Antony Lindsay said: "CW Sellors is a well-established British jewellery and watch business with strong foundations and a loyal customer base. Our focus now is on developing the business further whilst preserving the craftsmanship, expertise and customers relationships that have defined the brand for almost half a century."

Article continues below

Express.co.uk has reached out to River Island for comment.

News.title: 'Donald Trump's climbdown could be a ploy - but Iran unlikely to accept it'
News.content:
'Donald Trump's climbdown could be a ploy - but Iran unlikely to accept it'

US President Donald Trump's climbdown in the Iran war offers Tehran an off-ramp they are unlikely to accept - but it may also be a ploy to bide time to prepare for escalation

View 5 Images US President Donald Trump makes huge climbdown (Image: Getty Images)

Just weeks into this ill-planned, poorly justified and legally questionable conflict started by US President Donald Trump it had begun to look like it could morph into another ‘forever war.’ Despite Trump’s claim of winning, and not needing military help from allies he has spent the past year insulting on the world stage, it seemed impossible to know how long it will last.

‌



That it would be ‘weeks’ was beginning to look like optimism and that it would go down in history as the biggest and most seismic blunder felt on the world stage by an American President. But with his enormous and humiliating climbdown today Trump has claimed the US and Iran may be approaching “complete and total resolution of our hostilities in the Middle East.”

‌



The statement alone, despite the fact it is highly suspect, is a major climbdown for Trump and it will not be lost on the Iranian regime that he has blinked first. Casting further chaos into the mix Iran has repeated its claim that it is not talking to the US, a claim reiterated by Iranian news agencies close to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

‌



View 5 Images An Israeli self-propelled howitzer artillery gun fires rounds towards southern Lebanon as the Middle East conflict intensifies (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

So, for the moment it seems at best that Donald Trump has offered Tehran an off-ramp whilst crucially he is desperately in need of one for the United States as he needs a de-escalation. In a reduction of his threat to destroy “Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure” he has said he will hold off for a five day period.

View 5 Images Footage shows an Iranian ship engulfed in flames near Bandar Abbas following strikes linked to the ongoing US–Israeli campaign (Image: INSTAGRAM)

‌



There is no doubt that Iran believes it is winning this war, perhaps misguidedly, but they will be unable to go back to trusting Trump's word and will be guarded in the coming weeks and maybe months, still maintaining an aggressive posture towards the States, Israel and their allies.

Trump has tried to escalate his way out of this catastrophe, sending ground troops and threatening to blow up Iran’s energy infrastructure unless the Strait of Hormuz is opened. Meanwhile as the war slips into the 24th day he is has also insisted the war is already won - whilst the truth is that even opening up the Straits will not be the end of this crisis.

There is no doubt that the US and Israeli military has hugely degraded Iran’s military but the regime is still intact and the crisis gripping the Strait of Hormuz is not solved. It will need securing probably for years and if Trump does still launch a ‘boots on the ground’ operation it would lead to the inevitable mission creep and demands for more forces, ships, warplanes and escalation.

‌



View 5 Images Iran has been targeting places across the region, including the US Embassy headquarters in Baghdad (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

It has been rather like US General William Westmoreland insisting he could see ‘the light at the end of the tunnel’ as his delusion told America the US was winning the Vietnam War. He counted the Viet Cong death toll as a metric to judge victory, whilst insisting on sending more troops to the frontline, a perfect example of mission creep and escalation.

More recently war in Afghanistan went on for almost 20 years as the military and politicians insisted the country was being changed, that the Taliban was being degraded and defeated. Again, more troops were sent out and politicians insisted gun barrel persuasion was bringing human rights, even as the Taliban formed shadow governments in every province.

‌



View 5 Images Iranian Red Crescent clear blast rubble in Tehran (Image: AP)

Not only that, but the Taliban even told us they were doing this ten years previously, a senior commander meeting me in Pakistan saying his network had everything in place already. The world may breathe a sigh of relief that a major escalation has been stalled for now.

But the Strait of Hormuz crisis will not go away. One intelligence assessment seen this morning says: “Iran has turned its long coastline on the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint into a strategic asset against the United States and Israel.

Article continues below

“No matter how the Strait is reopened - whether through diplomatic accord or the threat or use of force - Iran’s regime has established a new normal in the Persian Gulf that leaves Tehran positioned to influence the global energy market.”
